ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-15-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Scott Righthand, Esq., appearing for the plaintiff. Plaintiff's counsel notified the court that this case has settled. The court set the case for an order to show cause regarding dismissal pursuant to CRC. 3.1385 on April 15, 2025 at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brittany Rogers, Esq. of Law Office of Scott Righthand, Tel. 415.544.0115, appearing in person for Plaintiff Nina Malayeri;
Carmen Gutman, Esq. of Cholakian & Associates, Tel. 650.871.9544, appearing via Zoom for Defendants AEG Presents SF LLC and AEG Live SF LLC.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT AEG PRESENTS LLC, AEG LIVE LLC, AND AEG LIVE SF LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants AEG LIVE LLC and AEG PRESENTS LLC's motions for summary judgment are denied as moot. On August 9, 2024, plaintiff dismissed these parties.

The court views the evidence in a light favorable to the party opposing summary judgment, "liberally construing her evidentiary submission while strictly scrutinizing defendants' own showing, and resolving any evidentiary doubts or ambiguities in plaintiff's favor." (Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 768.)

Defendant AEG LIVE SF LLC's ("Movant") motion for summary judgment is denied. Movant failed to maintain its burden of production. (CCP 437c(p)(2).) Movant shows that it changed its name on March 2, 2017, and it operated the concert venue under a new LLC monicker. (UMF 4-5.) But there is evidence in the record that movant entered into the operative security agreement, which included insurance obligations, and that agreement was in effect at the time of the accident. (Rogers Decl., Exs. C [Security Service Agreement]; D [Johnson Depo., 27:10-28:8]; E [Bernstein Depo., 10:15-11:20].) The cited evidence also shows that it was merely a name change and there was no asset/liability transfer. Movant therefore remains a proper party to this action pursuant to California Corporations Code section 2011(a)(1)(A) [causes of action may be enforced against dissolved corporation's insurance assets]. (See also Penasquitos, Inc. v. Superior Court (1991) 53 Cal.3d 1180, 1188 ["[S]ection 2010 permits parties to sue dissolved corporations"].) The court overrules movant's objections to the adduced evidence.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed form of order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Nguyen. (301/RCE)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2023 Continued To Dec-19-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Stay Proceedings Against Defendants Johnson Security Protection Johnson Security Protection Llc Genesis Protective Services And Genesis Protective Services Llc
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT JOHNSON PROTECTION SECURITY, INC., AEG PRESENTS LLC,, AEG PRESENTS SF LLC.'s Motion To Stay Proceedings Against Defendants Johnson Security Protection Johnson Security Protection Llc Genesis Protective Services And Genesis Protective Services Llc.

Defendants' motions for leave to withdraw answers and for stay are denied.

Plaintiff pleads that she was injured at a rap concert. Defendants (under their names and dba's) allegedly provide security at concerts. Confusion exists as to who was responsible for security at this concert.

Despite reaching back to 1929, defendants cite no case in which an answer was ordered withdrawn. The court suggests a person-most-knowledgeable deposition and/or consultation between counsel to resolve the confusion.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
